codeReview.summary: The feature adds normalization and deterministic fingerprinting, but the anchor-kind normalization is incompatible with the project’s established anchor vocabulary. That causes equivalent findings using existing provider values like added/deleted to hash differently instead of normalizing into the internal model the feature contract describes.

issues.description: `normalizeAnchorKind` only trims whitespace and defaults empty values to `new_line`, but it does not canonicalize the existing anchor kinds already used elsewhere in the codebase (`added`/`deleted`, and older provider payloads using `new`/`old`). The architecture expects provider findings to be normalized into one internal model before fingerprinting, yet semantically identical anchors with values like `added` versus `new_line` produce different persisted `anchor_kind` values and therefore different anchor fingerprints. This breaks deterministic normalization/deduplication for equivalent findings instead of unifying them.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, committed diff, mission guidance, skill, and shared-state files. The implementation is close, but it misses anchor-kind canonicalization: equivalent provider anchor labels are not normalized to one internal representation before persistence/fingerprinting, so the feature does not fully satisfy the normalization contract.
